overview:
## Discussion

This call is synchronous. The application is in a state ready to handle events when this method returns.

If the application isn’t running prior to calling [doc://com.apple.xcuiautomation/documentation/XCUIAutomation/XCUIApplication/activate()], it launches automatically.

If the application previously launched via [doc://com.apple.xcuiautomation/documentation/XCUIAutomation/XCUIApplication/launch()], the system supplies the launch arguments and environment variables from the original call to [doc://com.apple.xcuiautomation/documentation/XCUIAutomation/XCUIApplication/launch()] again.

Unlike [doc://com.apple.xcuiautomation/documentation/XCUIAutomation/XCUIApplication/launch()], a call to [doc://com.apple.xcuiautomation/documentation/XCUIAutomation/XCUIApplication/activate()] doesn’t terminate the existing instance if the application is already running.

The system reports any failure in the activation or launch sequence as a test failure and the test is halted at that point.

# activate()

**Instance Method**

Activates the application.

## Declaration

```swift
func activate()
```

## Discussion

This call is synchronous. The application is in a state ready to handle events when this method returns.

If the application isn’t running prior to calling [doc://com.apple.xcuiautomation/documentation/XCUIAutomation/XCUIApplication/activate()], it launches automatically.

If the application previously launched via [doc://com.apple.xcuiautomation/documentation/XCUIAutomation/XCUIApplication/launch()], the system supplies the launch arguments and environment variables from the original call to [doc://com.apple.xcuiautomation/documentation/XCUIAutomation/XCUIApplication/launch()] again.

Unlike [doc://com.apple.xcuiautomation/documentation/XCUIAutomation/XCUIApplication/launch()], a call to [doc://com.apple.xcuiautomation/documentation/XCUIAutomation/XCUIApplication/activate()] doesn’t terminate the existing instance if the application is already running.

The system reports any failure in the activation or launch sequence as a test failure and the test is halted at that point.
title: activate()
